insights.title: Narrative Fabrication
insights.summary: A false story can be built by combining staged normality with erased evidence.
insights.transfer_rule: Synthesize scene design, forensic absence, and timeline contradiction into one model.

cases.title: The Quiet Departure
cases.summary: A disappearance appears voluntary, but the scene contains signs of staging, erasure, and timeline mismatch. This case requires insights from both C001 and C002.
evidence: A coat is missing from the rack.
A train ticket is placed on the desk.
The coffee in the mug is still warm.
Security footage has a twelve-minute gap.
No fingerprints are visible on the train ticket.
detective_questions: What story does the scene appear to tell at first glance?
Which details seem too convenient for that story?
What does the warm coffee imply about timing?
Why would a ticket be present but fingerprint-free?
How do the camera gap and the calm room interact?
analysis_protocol: Separate raw observations from story-like interpretations.
Generate 2–3 explanations for each major clue before selecting any theory.
Only commit after combining scene design, forensic absence, and time logic.
scenarios: The resident left voluntarily for the train.
A second actor staged a voluntary departure narrative.
The resident planned a disappearance alone and cleaned everything.

evidence_meta.text: A coat is missing from the rack.
A train ticket is placed on the desk.
The coffee in the mug is still warm.
Security footage has a twelve-minute gap.
No fingerprints are visible on the train ticket.

cases.hidden_truth: A second actor staged the scene to simulate a voluntary departure.
cases.false_narrative: The resident calmly left for travel.

contradictions.description: Warm coffee implies recent presence — conflicts with a planned departure.
A handled ticket should carry prints — their absence signals cleaning.
A surveillance gap paired with a calm scene suggests hidden activity.

inference_traps.description: A visible train ticket does not by itself prove voluntary travel; planted travel evidence can be staged.
trap_keywords: left voluntarily because of the ticket

inference_traps.description: Warm coffee conflicts with a calm planned departure; treating it as normal misses the timeline contradiction.

required_concept_rules.name: Staged normality
required_concept_rules.description: Recognizes that the orderly room may have been arranged.

required_concept_rules.name: Forensic erasure
required_concept_rules.description: The fingerprint-free ticket is treated as suspicious.

required_concept_rules.name: Timeline contradiction
required_concept_rules.description: Warm coffee and camera gap break the voluntary departure story.

forbidden_patterns.description: Treating the fingerprint-free ticket as normal.
